summary:
The infant mortality rate is calculated using specific data from April births showing high early neonatal death counts; however, precise global comparisons are challenging due to differing socioeconomic factors impacting outcomes differently across regions. Based on this localized information, an approximate IMR can be derived while recognizing potential variations when applying broader statistical norms. 

The infant mortality rate appears around [calculated value], reflecting significant early losses among newborns within the first month, yet accounting for overall yearly fatality trends suggests higher risks during infancy necessitating targeted interventions focusing particularly on immediate postnatal care improvements given limited resources noted initially.
